description_long: This house consists of two connected rectangular volumes, each with a steeply pitched gable roof clad in dark gray material. The exterior walls are finished in a light wood or wood-like siding, giving the structure a modern yet natural appearance. One section features a large, open front with a wide window grid, suggesting a living area with ample natural light. The other section has a smaller, vertically oriented window, providing a more private space. The overall design is minimalistic, with clean lines and a lack of decorative elements. The house appears to be a single-story dwelling, suitable for a suburban or semi-rural neighborhood.
description: A modern, two-volume house with steep gable roofs, light wood siding, and large front windows, featuring a minimalistic design.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the two connected rectangular sections, the dark gable roofs, and the placement of a single vertical window.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the plain wooden siding and the steep gable ends of both sections, with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_2: Top-down view from the opposite side, again showing the roof layout and the vertical window on the left section.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the large, open window grid on one section and a smaller vertical window on the other, emphasizing the main entry and living area.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with one large picture window and one vertical window
attribute_entryway_design: Open front with a large window grid, no visible porch or decorative elements, minimalistic entry
